lines: Improves attack speed, evasion, and ranged accuracy. Rogues and Rangers lean on DEX for weapons and mobility skills.
Higher item levels scale base stats and requirements with dungeon depth tiers. Upgrades and drops respect your deepest cleared depth.
Fuels spell power and mana-heavy skills. Sorcerers rely on Magic for projectile and utility spells.
Common, Magic, Rare, and higher tiers unlock more affix rolls. Rarity color follows the inventory panel legend.
Increases melee damage and heavy-weapon effectiveness. Many weapons and skills require minimum STR to equip or rank up.
Raises maximum health and survivability. Paladins start with higher VIT; all classes benefit from extra life.

Leveled consumable stored in your bag.
Merge three stones of the same level at the blacksmith to create one stone of level +1.
Leveled consumable stored in your bag.
Merge three stones of the same level at the blacksmith to create one stone of level +1.
Upgrade Shards and Renew Stones drop from a separate resource pool on kills and chest opens. Tier chances depend on monster or chest type.

sections.heading: Shard Level
lines: Dropped resource level rolls uniformly from 1..max(1, floor(depth / 10)).
Spend gold (equal to the item sell price) plus an Upgrade Shard whose level is at least the target item level. Success raises item level and rescales stats.

sections.heading: Caps
bullets: Config max upgrade steps per item: 2
Depth cap at deepest cleared floor: item_level + 1 <= max(1, floor(depth / 10))
Example at depth 25: max item level 2

lines: Spend gold (sell price) plus a Renew Stone whose level is at least the item level. Random affixes reroll; unique fixed effects stay intact.
At the blacksmith merge tab, combine three leveled Upgrade Shards or Renew Stones of the same level into one stone of level +1.
Loot profiles point monsters, chests, and bosses at a treasure class (TC). Each attempt rolls success vs no-drop, then picks a weighted entry from the table.

sections.heading: Flow
bullets: Loot profile selects a treasure_class_id for the event.
Each attempt rolls success_weight vs no_drop_weight.
On success, one entry is picked by weight from the table.
Entries may be fixed items (item_def_id) or equipment templates (item_template_id).
